title: Prechristmas Drinks
description:
Dear R-Ladies

It was so wonderful to meet many of you at our first meetup this Monday!

We tought it would be nice to have a little informal get together in December so we don‘t have to wait two months until we meet again.

There will be no agenda, the plan is to get hot drinks at the Christmas market at Landesmuesum.

We are looking forward to it!

Best,
Seraphina&Luisa

title: R-Ladies Zürich: first meetup
description:
Dear R-Ladies!

We are happy to announce that the first event of the newly founded R-Ladies chapter in Zürich will take place in November.

Our first meeting is about getting to know each other. We will have three short talks where you‘ll get to know more about three R-Ladies and their work, and socializing parts between the talks and at the end where you have time to get to know each other.

We can‘t wait to meet you!
Seraphina & Luisa

title: Personalize your own ggplot theme
description:
Hello everybody!
We're happy to announce our next event on Thursday, December 1st, 2022, form 18:30 to 19:30 (with open end socializing).

The meeting will take place in person in room KOL-E-18 in the main building of Universität Zürich (KOL).

This time, we'll learn something more about ggplot and how to create a personalized theme with the help of some simple examples / a guided tutorial.

You can find the materials for the workshop here:
https://github.com/rladies-zurich/ggplot_theme

Don't forget to bring your own laptop and charger. Wi-Fi will be provided at the event location.

The schedule will be as follows:
18:00 - 18:30 possibility to get support for installing the packages and getting the material necessary for the workshop. If you are an R-beginner and need any type of tech support, don't hesitate to ask us!
18:30 - 18:40 welcome and some ice-breaker questions
18:45 - 19:30 workshop
19:30 onwards: socializing with some drinks at bQm.

We're very much looking forward to seeing you there!
Céline, Seraphina and Luisa

title: Creative CVs with RMarkdown
description:
What is the best way to show your future employer that you are proficient with RMarkdown? By creating your CV with RMarkdown!

Join us for our event in September where we will follow an online tutorial to create our very own RMarkdown CVs. No prior knowledge required. If you don‘t have R and RStudio installed, we recommend that you come at 18:00 so we can help you install it.

**IMPORTANT**: This is an **interactive** **workshop**. We kindly ask you to **bring your own laptop and charger.** Internet access will be provided at the event location.
Materials for the tutorial (templates, list of necessary packages) are available here: [https://github.com/CST333/CV_with_vitae](https://github.com/CST333/CV_with_vitae)

Program:
18:00 - 18:30 Technical support to install R, RStudio and the packages necessary for the event
18:30 - 19:00 Welcome + input presentation by Daniela Gunz from the UZH Career Center
19:00 - 20:00 Tutorial!
20:00 onward Socializing :)

We can‘t wait to meet you!
Luisa, Céline and Seraphina

title: R-Ladies Zürich @ Zurich R User Group
description:
Hi!

Do you plan to attend the next R User Group Event? Let‘s enjoy the short walk from Zürich HB to the event location together.

We meet at the official „Treffpunkt“ in the main hall on the ground level.

ATTENTION: you have to register for the R User Group Event separately!! Register now, there is a limited number of participants.

We are looking forward to the Zurich R User group event, and to many R-Ladies showing up!

Best,
Seraphina, Céline and Luisa

Link to the R User Group Event:
https://meetu.ps/e/L8NRl/TJrF1/i

title: R-Ladies Zürich: second meetup
description:
Dear R-Ladies Zürich

Our second meetup will be a viRtual pub quiz! Grab a refreshing beverage at the end of your day and enjoy some exciting questions from the coziness of your couch.

We will meet on zoom on Tuesday, Feb 1st 2022, 7-8:30 pm.

Be sure to RSVP to receive the link to the meeting ;)

We are looking forward to seeing you there!
Seraphina & Luisa

title: Guess Who? - R Packages Edition
description:
How well do you know your favorite R packages? Curious to explore the vast toolkit that R has to offer?

Join us on **Thursday, December 14th at 6:30 pm** for a fun game evening and networking event. **Location: Oliver Twist Pub, Rindermarkt 6, 8001 Zurich**.
We'll provide some snacks and beverages, just come as you are and share your experience and questions!

We will bring a unique twist to the classic "*Guess Who?*" game by diving into the realm of R packages. Whether you're a professional R user or just starting your journey, this event promises to be a delightful exploration of the most fascinating R packages.

We're very much looking forward to seeing you there :)
Céline, Luisa, Seraphina and Elena

title: Let's go to the Zurich R User Group event together!
description:
Do you want to go to the Zurich R User Group event tonight but are not sure who to go with (and you don't want to go alone)? Look no further!
Let's meet at the **Treffpunkt at Zurich main station at 17:55** and go together :)
You'll find us under the big clock with a sign. We'll leave at 18:00 and walk to the event location.

Don't forget to sign up for the event on the Zurich RUG meetup page:
[https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/events/295200311/](https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/events/295200311/)

title: Zero to Quarto: Build your first Quarto website
description:
**Zero to Quarto: build your first Quarto website!**

**Quarto** is one of the most discussed topics in the R community for the past year. In the posit conf 2023 that just happened a few weeks ago, there were 23 (!) talks on this **open-source publishing system**. You can build a personal or organisation website; a course or workshop website; a blog; a scientific report; a presentation; all with the possibility to display and execute code (R, Python, Julia), and for free.

If you:

* don't know what quarto is yet, and want to find out more
* heard of quarto somewhere, but don't know where to start
* are an expert in quarto and want to share your experience

then this workshop is for you!

Join us and our speaker **Chi Zhang** on **Monday, October 23rd** for this exciting event.

**No previous experience with Quarto is required**, just bring your laptop and charger and join us for this creative programming experience ;-)

Wi-Fi will be provided at the event location.

Materials for the workshop can be downloaded or forked from
[https://andreaczhang.github.io/workshop-02quarto/](https://andreaczhang.github.io/workshop-02quarto/)

Program:
18:00 - 18:30 possibility to get support for installing the packages and getting the material necessary for the workshop. If you are an R-beginner and need any type of tech support, don't hesitate to ask us!
18:30 - 18:40 welcome and some ice-breaker questions
18:45 - 19:30 workshop
19:30 onwards: socializing with some drinks at TBD

Event location: KO2-F-153 at UZH Campus

We're very much looking forward to seeing you there!
Céline, Seraphina, Elena and Luisa

title: oRganized: how do you structure your R-work?
description:
How do you oRganize your R work?

Let's talk tools, workflows, strategies and share how we structure our work with R!

Join us on **Wednesday, August 16th** for an **informal networking event** around this topic.

We'll meet at **18:00 in front of the Landesmuseum** and find a spot at Platzpitz park. We'll provide some snacks and beverages, just come as you are and share your experience and questions!

In case of bad weather we'll update you about an alternative location.

We're very much looking forward to seeing you there :)
Céline, Seraphina and Luisa

title: Let's go to the Zurich R User Group event together!
description:
Do you want to go to the Zurich R User Group event tonight but are not sure who to go with (and you don't want to go alone)? Look no further!

Let's meet at the **Treffpunkt at Zurich main station at 17:55** and go together :)

You'll find us under the big clock with a sign. We'll leave at 18:00 and take the tram to the event location.

Don't forget to sign up for the event on the Zurich RUG meetup page:
[https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/events/293701902/](https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/events/293701902/)

title: Build your first web app with R Shiny
description:
Get ready for our next workshop on **building web apps with R Shiny**!

We're thrilled to have a very special speaker for this session: **Olajoke Oladipo** will guide us through the exciting world of Shiny apps, and you will be able to get practical help with building your (first?) app.

**No previous experience with Shiny apps is required**, just bring your laptop and charger and join us for this creative programming experience ;-)
Wi-Fi will be provided at the event location.

Materials for the workshop can be downloaded or forked from [https://github.com/rladies-zurich/shiny101](https://github.com/rladies-zurich/shiny101)

Program:
18:00 - 18:30 possibility to get support for installing the packages and getting the material necessary for the workshop. If you are an R-beginner and need any type of tech support, don't hesitate to ask us!
18:30 - 18:40 welcome and some ice-breaker questions
18:45 - 19:30 workshop
19:30 onwards: socializing with some drinks at ["Kleine Freiheit"](https://kleinefreiheit.ch)

Event location: room KO2-F-153 at Rämistrasse 71 · Zürich

We're very much looking forward to seeing you there!
Céline, Seraphina and Luisa

title: R-Stories: Women from the R community
description:
On International Women's Day, we want to dedicate an event to **hearing stories and experiences from women in the R community**.

Join us for an exciting **panel discussion** featuring some very interesting **guests**
\- Andrea Schnell \(Statistisches Amt Kanton Zürich\)
\- Dr\. Beatrice Marti \(Hydrosolutions Ltd\.\)
\- Dr\. Benita Combet \(UZH\, Institute for Sociology\)
\- Dr\. Mirka Henninger \(UZH\, Department of Psychology\)
and **moderator** Dr. Andreea Loredana Cretu (BEI Program).

The event will take place on **March 8th, 2023, 18:30 - 20:00** in room **KO2-F-152 EV** in the main building of the university of Zürich and will be followed by a small apero.

Program:
18:30 - 18:45 Welcome and introduction of panelists
18:45 - 19:30 Panel discussion
19:30 - 19:50 Open discussion with public
19:50 - 20:00 Wrap up and R giveaways
20:00 < Apero

This is a j**oint event of R-Ladies Zürich and the R User Group Zürich**.
Anyone is welcome to join!

Looking forward to seeing many of you there!

title: Pub Night
description:
Save the date for our next socializing event. No agenda, the plan is to grab a drink at a pub, have a chat and get to know the members of this meetup group.

Location UPDATE:
We will meet at Zürich HB - Treffpunkt and then go to Norden Bar, Ankerstrasse 123, 8004 Zürich

title: Winter Networking
description:
R-Ladies Zürich is looking forward to the last community networking event of 2024!
Join us for some hot beverages and enjoy a winter evening with us.

We'll meet at the Treffpunkt at Zürich HB at 18:30 and head to a Christmas market afterwards.
We'll have a sign with "R-Ladies Zürich", so you will recognise us.

Looking forward to seeing you!

title: Visualising data with ggplot2 – Preparing figures for publication and outreach
description:
The visualisations we use to present our findings matter as much as the text, if not more. We will walk through the steps to create beautiful figures that can make posters, talks, and publications shine. If you can picture a cool way to visualize data, it can probably be achieved with ggplot2!

This workshop is presented by Ryssa. She is a postdoc in Social Brain Sciences at ETH Zurich. Her research focuses on how the brain and body mediate the development of intergenerational social bonds, and how this can reduce loneliness for people of all ages.

**Programm:**
18:30-20:15 workshop
20:15 - onwards socializing and drinks

**Location:**
University of Zurich (KOL-H-309)

We are looking forward to having a great evening together!

Seraphina, Céline, Luisa, Elena and Ryssa!

title: Grow Your Career In Tech: #IamRemarkable Workshop
description:
Are you battling with imposter syndrome at work? Struggling to find the confidence to speak up and acknowledge your worth? Join the **#IamRemarkable workshop** on Thursday, **September 26th 2024**.

#IAmRemarkable is a global movement founded by Google that empowers everyone to celebrate their achievements in the workplace and beyond.
Over 600k people joined the initiative and 82% of them reported a confidence boost thanks to the insights and the activities shared in the workshop.

In this experience, you will be guided by **trained facilitator Clelia Calabrò**, who is passionate about adaptation to change and transformation.

Find more info on the [#IamRemarkable](https://www.rmrkblty.org/iamremarkable) initiative.

Ready to grow your career in tech with #IamRemarkable? Save your seat now, we canʼt wait to meeting you soon!

This event to open to people of all gender identities.

Céline, Elena, Luisa, Seraphina - and Clelia!

title: let‘s GIT to it
description:
What if you could turn back time? With git, an open source software for version control, you can do that - at least for your code, should the need arise.

In our next workshop, Dominique and Nicolas from ipt (https://ipt.ch/) will teach you how to use git to version control your code, and to effectively collaborate with others on that code.

**Workshop Preparation:**
No previous experience with git required. Please bring a (charged) laptop with the following components installed:

* Git ([Download Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads)): Download and install Git, making a note of where on your computer you install it.
* R ([Download R](https://cloud.r-project.org/)): Download and Install R (if not already installed).
* RStudio ([Download RStudio Desktop](https://www.rstudio.com/products/RStudio/#Desktop)): Download and Install RStudio (if not already installed)
* GitHub account: On [GitHub](https://github.com/) create yourself a free GitHub account.

(Source:[https://www.geo.uzh.ch](https://www.geo.uzh.ch/microsite/reproducible_research/post/rr-rstudio-git/))

If you would like some help installing, use the time slot from 17:30 - 18:00. We are happy to help you out!

During the workshop, you will be asked to work with the following repository: https://github.com/iptch/2023-03-git-techbier

**Program:**
17:30 - 18:00 possibility to get support for installing RStudio and git
18:00 - 18:10 welcome and ice-breaker questions
18:15 - 20:15 workshop
20:15 onwards: socializing & sponsored apéro

**Location:**
Raum SI 122
KV Business School Zürich AG
Bildungszentrum Sihlpost
Sihlpostgasse 2
8004 Zürich

We are very excited to be able to host this workshop for you together with ipt, and we look forward to chatting to you after the event at the apéro!
Seraphina, Céline, Luisa

title: R-Stories: The Role of Gender in Data Collection and Analysis
description:
A day before International Women's Day, we dedicate a **joint event of R-Ladies Zürich and the [Zurich R User Group](https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/)** to hearing about experiences and considerations for when collecting and analysing data on gender and diversity.

Join us for an exciting panel discussion moderated by Luisa Barbanti (Zurich Data Scientists), featuring some very interesting guests:

* Luisa Coppola (ABB Schweiz AG)
* Luca M. Leisten (ETH Zurich)
* Almut Scherer (SCQM)
* Christina Seyler (UZH),
* and others.

The event will take place on **March 7th, 2023, 18:30 - 20:00** in room **KOL-E-13 EV** in the main building of the university of Zürich and will be followed by a networking apero.

Program:
18:30 - 18:45 Welcome and introduction of panellists
18:45 - 19:30 Panel discussion
19:30 - 19:50 Open discussion with audience
19:50 - 20:00 Wrap up
20:00 < Apero, first round sponsored by the Zurich R User Group

We welcome people of all sexual orientations and gender identities and are looking forward to lively discussions!

Last but not least, we ask you to only sign up either through R-Ladies Zürich or the [Zurich R User Group](https://www.meetup.com/zurich-r-user-group/) so that we can avoid double-booking.

title: Let's catch up!
description:
NetwoRking: Let’s Catch Up!
Join us for a casual get-together where you can meet like-minded people, chat about R, and discuss career-related topics.
R-Ladies will provide thought-provoking questions to spark great conversations.

We will meet on **Wednesday, April 9th at 18:30** close to Zurich Main Station.
UPDATE: we meet at [Norden Bar](https://maps.app.goo.gl/fWvCTskbYELwU8hx5)

Come for the connections, stay for the insights!

Looking forward to seeing you :-)
